description.content:
The Teams specification documents internal APIs exercised by the Microsoft Teams admin center
(`admin.teams.microsoft.com`) during an exhaustive crawl of the left nav, detail pages, and
report interactions with a real authenticated Edge session.

The current capture set confirmed multiple distinct backend families behind the portal, including:

  - same-origin admin portal APIs on `admin.teams.microsoft.com` and `noneu-admin.teams.microsoft.com`
  - cross-origin Teams platform calls on `teams.microsoft.com/api`
  - user, policy, telephony, CQD, template, and app-instance lookups on
    `api.interfaces.records.teams.microsoft.com`
  - frontline orchestration, device management, monetization, monitoring, reports, app catalog,
    CQD data, and planning backends on additional Teams- and Office-hosted services

The retained evidence includes explicit notes for feature-, data-, or permission-gated surfaces
such as Silent Tests, inactive Teams rule details, workforce hierarchy operations, and device
tenant metadata that did not fully resolve in the capture tenant.

Most cross-origin calls were observed with a browser-acquired bearer token and `X-Requested-With`
header. `POST /api/log` was only observed as a same-origin request from the admin portal itself and
should be treated as portal-context dependent rather than a general-purpose external API.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best-efforts basis and may not be
100% accurate.
